title: Moulton allegedly knocks phone from questioner’s hand after being asked about Graham Platner
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Rep. Seth Moulton allegedly knocked a phone from a person's hand during a Massachusetts event, sparking a dispute with Fox News.
Moulton's campaign claims the person was a 'paid political tracker' from America Rising, not a reporter as initially suggested by Fox News.
Fox News denies paying the person and acknowledged they are not a reporter after Moulton's campaign disputed the claim.

title: Durbin homers to break tie in the Red Sox’s 6-3 win over the Yankees
author: The Associated Press
bullets: Caleb Durbin's two-run homer broke a tie in the fifth inning, leading the Red Sox to a 6-3 win over the Yankees.
Connelly Early pitched six innings for Boston, allowing two runs, contributing to his seventh win of the season.
Yankees starter Cam Schlittler struck out nine, allowing four unearned runs as New York committed four errors in the game.

title: 3 injured when car crashes into Newburyport restaurant
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A 2024 BMW X3 crashed into the Black Cow restaurant in Newburyport, injuring three customers on Thursday evening.
The driver, a 67-year-old woman from Groveland, was uninjured and authorities don't suspect alcohol as a factor.
Two men were hospitalized, and repairs are needed for the bar area, but the restaurant remains open.

title: Suspect in custody after man, 58, fatally stabbed in Wellesley park
author: Audrey Cooney
bullets: A 26-year-old man was arrested after a 58-year-old man was fatally stabbed at Phillips Park in Wellesley on Thursday.
The victim, found with a stab wound, was pronounced dead at Newton Wellesley Hospital, according to Norfolk County DA.
James Floyd Bennett III, of Wellesley, was arrested and expected to be arraigned in Dedham District Court on Friday.

title: Framingham police will not renew Flock Safety contract after months of resident opposition
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Framingham Police Department will not renew its contract with Flock Safety for automated license plate readers.
Residents raised privacy concerns, opposing the system since February, leading to the decision to end the contract.
Neighboring communities like Cambridge and Natick have also stopped using Flock due to data sharing and oversight worries.

title: ‘Over a foot of manure’: Horses, mules among animals rescued from Western Mass. property, MSPCA says
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: MSPCA-Angell rescued 13 animals from a Western Massachusetts property due to unsanitary conditions including over a foot of manure.
Rescued animals include seven horses, two mules, a donkey, two geese, and a cockatoo, many underweight with overgrown hooves.
The animals are now at MSPCA-Angell at Nevins Farm for treatment and adoption, as the MSPCA manages over 35 farm animals.

title: 10 players Bruins could target in 1st round of 2026 NHL Draft
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bruins hold eight picks in the 2026 NHL Draft, including No. 23 overall in the first round.
Prospects like Tommy Bleyl and Nikita Klepov are among potential first-round targets for the Bruins.
Bruins lost anticipated high draft pick from Toronto due to Maple Leafs' lottery victory, delaying the pick to 2028.

title: Coyote warning issued in Beverly after dog attacked on trail
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Beverly police issued a warning after a coyote attacked a dog at the Fox Hill Trail on Tall Tree Drive.
The attack occurred near a coyote den with pups, prompting safety advice from Beverly police.
Residents urged to leash dogs, avoid coyote dens, and report aggressive coyote behavior in wooded areas.

title: Preserving a vehicle’s computer settings while replacing a battery
author: John Paul
bullets: John Paul of AAA Northeast advises against changing a car's battery with the engine running, recommending alternative methods.
For maintaining a vehicle's computer memory, use advanced battery chargers or a jump pack during battery replacement.
In case of electronic car key deactivation, use another activated key or the InControl Remote App for reactivation.

title: Nationwide rental listings are most affordable in years, Zillow says. In Boston? Not quite.
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Zillow reports 74% of national rental listings are affordable, but Boston's rate is only 50.2%.
Boston renters spend 30.6% of income on new rentals; typical rent is $3,211, fourth-highest nationally.
Boston's annual rent increase is 2.5%, lower than New York and San Francisco, but stabilization remains challenging.
